title: UN Secretary-General warns Strait of Hormuz closure strangling global economy
summary: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres declared that the closure of the Strait of Hormuz constitutes the biggest energy crisis in history, urging dialogue as consequences grow 'dramatically worse with each passing hour.'
contents:
## UN Chief Warns of Unprecedented Energy Crisis

### Secretary-General Statement
UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres published a statement on X on Friday declaring that the closure of the Strait of Hormuz now "strangles the global economy." The statement called for immediate dialogue to prevent further escalation.

### IEA Assessment
International Energy Agency chief Fatih Birol reinforced the urgency at a meeting at the IEA's Paris headquarters, stating: "The world faces the biggest energy crisis in history." The assessment reflects the unprecedented scale of disruption caused by the simultaneous closure of the strait and the US naval blockade of Iranian ports.

### Global Impact
The Strait of Hormuz carries approximately 20% of the world's oil and gas supplies. Its closure since the beginning of the US-Iran conflict on February 28 has driven oil prices above $100 per barrel, with Brent crude briefly touching $126 per barrel on Thursday.

### Call for Action
"The consequences of the Middle East crisis grow dramatically worse with each passing hour," Guterres wrote. "Now stands as the time for dialogue, for solutions that pull us back from the brink." China's UN ambassador Fu Cong separately called the reopening of the strait an "urgent necessity."

### Diplomatic Context
The statement arrived as the US-Iran ceasefire, in place since April 7, appeared increasingly fragile, with Trump expressing dissatisfaction with Iran's latest peace proposal and Iran threatening "long and painful strikes" if the US renews attacks.
